name: energy_systems_workplace_injury_spike_millhaven_ind_col
premise: An internal crisis team convened after contradictory signals hit leadership from operations, legal, and external partners at once. In Energy Systems, the situation tagged internally around energy systems workplace injury spike millhaven ind col has converged with local pressure in the Millhaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Engineering says a full fix needs a maintenance window; operations says waiting will predictably increase harm in the interim. An internal red-team note and a customer ticket describe the same failure mode with incompatible timelines. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The organization’s past incidents make individual feel morally loud today, while collective is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Hector Patel
agents.identity: I am Hector Patel, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Energy Systems. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat collective as disposable collateral every time individual spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Zara Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Zara Vasquez, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Energy Systems. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so individual cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes collective in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on collective.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on collective for immediate traction on individual.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Hector Patel must choose under individual versus collective. What does Hector Patel do?
